body:
## Plan ready — BIN-66 Worldbuilding Panel

Implementation plan written and posted to the issue description.

**Summary:**
- 3 new files: `WorldbuildingPanel` component + CSS, `src/types/worldbuilding.ts`, `src/lib/worldbuilding.ts`
- 2 modified files: `page.tsx` (replace Notes placeholder, add `selectedSceneId` state), `page.module.css`
- Entity types: Character, Location, Lore — stored in `story-{id}-worldbuilding-entities` localStorage key
- Entity-scene links stored in `story-{id}-worldbuilding-links`
- Panel collapsible with state preserved; search + tab filter + link/unlink per scene
- Active scene defaults to first scene; selectable via scene click handler

Assign to: Code Monkey

## Update
Design brief for [BIN-66](/BIN/issues/BIN-66), aligned to current editor architecture and grayscale token system.

## UX goal
Keep drafting primary while making world context instantly available: writers should add/find/link character, location, and lore entities without leaving the editor or losing writing momentum.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: active drafting canvas and story title.
2. Secondary: worldbuilding panel with quick search, entity list, and pinned-for-scene entities.
3. Tertiary: entity metadata/details and low-frequency actions (edit/delete).

## Layout and components
- In editor mode, keep the existing two-column pattern and convert the current right sidebar “Notes” area into a dedicated “Worldbuilding” panel.
- Panel structure: header (Worldbuilding label + collapse toggle), search input, segmented entity type tabs (Character, Location, Lore), entity list, and a scene-linked section (“Linked to this scene”).
- Scene context area should support one-click link/unlink with clear affordances (checkbox/chip/button pattern) and lightweight inline preview.
- Preserve corkboard mode layout; worldbuilding remains editor-contextual rather than global full-screen.

## Interaction states
- Idle: show entity list by selected type and current scene-linked entities.
- Loading: skeleton rows in panel list and disabled link controls while preserving visible editor text.
- Empty: friendly prompt per section (e.g., “No characters yet. Create one to start linking context.”).
- Error: inline non-blocking error strip inside panel with retry action; editor must remain usable.
- Success: subtle confirmation on link/unlink/create (brief text or icon state change), avoid disruptive toasts.

## Accessibility
- Full keyboard flow: tab into panel, arrow/tab through entities, Enter/Space to link/unlink, Esc to close modal/detail overlays.
- Maintain visible focus rings consistent with existing controls and avoid focus loss when collapsing/expanding panel.
- Ensure contrast uses approved grayscale combinations that meet WCAG AA for body text and controls.
- Use plain-language labels (e.g., “Linked to scene”) and avoid ambiguous icon-only actions.

## Visual style
- Follow existing grayscale tokens from `src/styles/tokens.css`; no new accent palette required for MVP.
- Keep editor dominant: white drafting surface, polar/fog backgrounds in side panel, fashion token borders/dividers.
- Typography should match current app hierarchy: sans for UI controls, serif only in writing canvas content.
- Keep panel visually quiet: compact rows, clear spacing rhythm, minimal decoration.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024px): persistent right panel (current ~300px pattern), collapsible to icon/rail state.
- Tablet (768-1023px): default collapsed panel with explicit open action; overlay/drawer behavior acceptable to protect text width.
- Mobile (<768px): panel should not permanently consume width; use slide-over or bottom-sheet access with quick open/close and retained editor focus.

## Codebase alignment
- Reviewed existing route/layout/component primitives and styling: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`, `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`, `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`, `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`, `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx`, and `src/styles/tokens.css`.
- Brief is aligned with current editor/corkboard split and existing sidebar slot in editor mode.

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse: current editor shell, title input pattern, existing sidebar container behavior, tokenized spacing/colors, and current focus/interactive styling conventions.
- New build: worldbuilding panel internals (entity tabs, list rows, linked-entity context block, collapse behavior, and entity create/edit micro-surfaces).

## Feasibility notes
- Current story editor is a single textarea without scene-level drafting context in UI; linking interactions may need a minimal “active scene” concept to avoid ambiguity.
- Scene data currently comes from local scene helpers; entity-link state must be designed to degrade gracefully if persistence is delayed.
- Collapse behavior should preserve unsaved text and panel filter/search state in-session.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Anchor worldbuilding UI inside the existing editor sidebar region in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` and `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` to avoid structural churn.
- Keep `ViewModeSwitch` behavior unchanged; this ticket should not alter corkboard navigation patterns.
- Respect existing token primitives in `src/styles/tokens.css` (grayscale, spacing, borders, focus outlines).
- “Quick-capture must feel fast”: creation affordance should be one-click visible from the panel header/list context.
- “Modes should be one-click”: linking/unlinking must be direct from list rows without multistep dialog dependency.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Worldbuilding is a competitive area, but we should differentiate by embedding just-enough context in the drafting surface (linked entities in-scene) instead of forcing writers into a separate encyclopedia workflow.

## Competitor evidence
- Campfire is strongly positioned around deep worldbuilding modules, proving demand and setting a benchmark for entity modeling: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Living Writer emphasizes cleaner drafting and structure flow, which supports our strategy to keep worldbuilding contextual and lightweight: https://livingwriter.com/features
- Inkitt’s strength is community/publishing rather than deep in-editor lore tooling, so this can be a clear product distinction for serious fiction drafting: https://writer.inkitt.com

## Risks / trade-offs
- Scope creep into full wiki/map systems can derail MVP.
- Side panel clutter can interrupt writing flow.
- Entity schema decisions now may constrain future expansion.

## Recommendation
Approve. Keep strict MVP boundaries (character/location/lore only) and require Design to define collapse behavior and glanceable in-scene context so writing remains primary.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
